payload.source_text: High Level Description
The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.name: Family Participation
payload.description: Defines the roles, relationships, and reciprocal responsibilities of family members within the household economy, establishing who may participate and the nature of their accountability to one another.

payload.name: Earning Entitlement
payload.description: Establishes the enterprise commitment to value-based compensation, defining what constitutes earnable value, the structures through which it is earned, and the child's right to claim what has been earned.

payload.name: Performance Transparency
payload.description: Governs the enterprise obligation to provide visibility into opportunity, achievement, and outcomes, ensuring participants can see what is available, track their progress, and understand their standing.

payload.name: Parental Accountability
payload.description: Establishes parental responsibility for oversight, financial execution, and governance of the household earning system, including the obligation to monitor, report, and discharge payment commitments.

payload.name: Record Retention Obligation
payload.description: Defines the enterprise commitment to maintain historical records over time for auditability, pattern analysis, and long-term accountability across all earning and payment activities.

payload.name: Task Obligation Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for defining, maintaining, and making available discrete obligations that carry monetary value, governing what work is offered within the household economy and how such obligations are structured and presented to participants.

payload.name: Participation Entitlement
payload.description: Business responsibility for governing how participants claim and fulfill obligations, establishing accountability for completion and linking performed work to the individual who executed it, ensuring proper attribution of effort within the household economy.

payload.name: Earnings Settlement Record
payload.description: Business responsibility for establishing and presenting the financial position resulting from completed obligations, aggregating value earned within defined cycles, and providing visibility to both earners and those responsible for disbursement to enable settlement transactions.

payload.name: Performance Record Accountability
payload.description: Business responsibility for preserving authoritative evidence of completed obligations across time, ensuring historical fulfillment data remains accessible for review, audit, and long-term accountability purposes beyond immediate settlement cycles.

payload.name: Operational Cycle Governance
payload.description: Business responsibility for establishing temporal boundaries that govern when obligations refresh, when settlement periods conclude, and how the household economy transitions between operational states to maintain rhythm and predictability.

payload.name: Task Lifecycle Model
payload.description: Logical structure and state transitions governing task definitions from creation through availability, claiming, and completion, including temporal state reset behavior at weekly boundaries.

payload.name: Task Completion Attribution Model
payload.description: Logical structure linking task completions to specific child entities, enforcing single-child association constraints and maintaining historical completion records for audit and reporting purposes.

payload.name: Earnings Derivation Logic
payload.description: Logical process for deriving weekly monetary aggregates per child based on completed task monetary values, including temporal aggregation boundaries and accumulation rules.

payload.name: Parent Supervisory Visibility Model
payload.description: Logical role-based access model enabling parent actors to view aggregated completion and earnings data across children, create task definitions, and access reporting functions within their supervisory scope.

payload.name: Child Task Interaction Model
payload.description: Logical role and filtering rules governing child actor access to task subsets based on availability and completion status, including task claiming authorization and earnings visibility within temporal scope.

payload.name: Operating System Platform
payload.description: Physical deployment target and compatibility constraints governing the operating system platform on which the system will be built and deployed.

payload.name: Multi-Platform Deployment Configuration
payload.description: Detailed specifications for platform-specific deployment constraints governing iOS mobile and Windows desktop runtime environments, including precise version requirements and configuration parameters necessary for cross-platform implementation.

payload.name: User Interface Actor Specification
payload.description: Precise interface specifications defining Parent and Child actor interactions, access controls, and role-based capabilities including task definition interfaces, completion workflows, report access screens, and read-only status viewing components with detailed permission boundaries.

payload.name: Weekly Cycle Timing Specification
payload.description: Detailed timing constraints governing weekly operational cycle boundaries, including precise reset timing parameters, cycle initiation sequencing, and earnings total access event timing specifications that coordinate task availability and reporting intervals.
